name: Sapayoidae
description: Sapayoidae is a monotypic family of passerine birds containing a single enigmatic species from Central and South America. Its evolutionary affinities were long debated before molecular studies placed it within the Old World suboscines.

children.name: Sapayoa
children.description: Sapayoa is a monotypic genus containing the sapayoa, a distinctive passerine restricted to lowland rainforests of Panama and northwestern South America.

children.name: Sapayoa aenigma
children.description: The sapayoa is a medium-sized olive-yellow passerine found in humid lowland forests from Panama to Ecuador. Its name reflects the long-standing mystery of its evolutionary relationships.
